brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Yes) with valid weighted-average calculation and correct causal inference; identifies two causal pathways (smallpox protection vs. vaccination reaction harm) and explains the net mechanism, going well beyond surface; proactively notes that vaccination is the root node so observational probabilities equal interventional ones, showing awareness of a key confounding pitfall; provides a concrete numerical sanity check at 50% vaccination rate but does not test boundary conditions or re-derive from an alternate angle; frames conclusions as holding 'in this hypothetical world' but does not articulate what assumption changes would flip the result; identifies the two competing pathways and explains why the net effect is negative but does not consider alternative interpretations of the causal structure; every numerical step is shown with full arithmetic and the reasoning chain is fully reproducible by a third party.
